resources.string: Grant camera permission to capture image
Browse media in Chimera. Android 14+ restricts access; please choose Allow all to view everything.

This project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0.\nBy using this software, you agree to the terms of the license, which\ninclude conditions for copying, distribution, and modification.
For the complete privacy policy, disclaimer, and license information, please visit our project page: \nhttps://github.com/ReRokutosei/Chimera
Please read and agree to our terms before using the app
Photo access is required to browse images
Clear selected image list from main page after stitching (does not delete local files)
Choose the direction of preview images before stitching
Use Storage Access Framework to select images without permission
Your device uses this picker by default
Use multithreading to process images in parallel and improve stitching speed
Raise memory threshold to 80% of device\'s total RAM
No valid image result was generated
Please select two or more images
Resolution exceeds limit!\nPlease select PNG format in settings
One or more images could not be loaded.
Image dimensions could not be read.
The stitched image is too large for this device or output format.
There is not enough memory to create the stitched image.
Stitching failed unexpectedly. Check the log for details.

This app is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranties, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. 

Use at Your Own Risk: 
All risks arising from the use of this app are borne by you. We do not warrant the accuracy or completeness of the app's functions. 

Limitation of Liability: 
In no event shall we be liable for any damages (including indirect or consequential damages) resulting from the use or inability to use this app. 

Service Availability: 
We do not guarantee that the app will be uninterrupted or error-free. The app may experience service interruptions due to technical updates, maintenance, or other force majeure events.

We do not collect personal information. 
You can use this app without providing any personal data. 
We do not share your personal data. 
Any revisions to this Privacy Policy will be published on the project’s GitHub repository and shall become effective with the corresponding app update, without prior or separate notification.
4-cell cutting completed. Please check your gallery.
9-cell cutting completed. Please check your gallery.
No images are available to cut.
An image could not be loaded for cutting.
The selected cut grid is invalid.
An image could not be cut. Check the log for details.
Cutting failed unexpectedly. Check the log for details.
The image could not be encoded.
The image could not be written to the gallery.
Saving failed unexpectedly. Check the log for details.

Places images in the selected horizontal or vertical order without using overlay mode. 
Image spacing can be set from 0 to 50 px. In vertical mode, scaling aligns image widths; in horizontal mode, it aligns image heights. 

Minimum: Scale each image proportionally to the smallest alignment dimension in the current selection. 

Original: Keep each image at its decoded dimensions. Images with different alignment dimensions are centered. 

Maximum: Scale each image proportionally to the largest alignment dimension in the current selection. This mode can require substantially more memory.

Keeps the first image in full, then appends a trailing section from each subsequent image. In essence, it refers to the elongated composite images commonly seen in film and television subtitling. This can be used to combine screenshots whose relevant content is near the bottom or right edge. \n\nThe ratio controls how much of each subsequent image is retained. A larger ratio produces a larger output and may require more memory.

Output dimensions are checked against encoder and device limits. 

JPEG: Each output dimension is limited to 65,535 pixels. 

WebP: Each output dimension is limited to 16,383 pixels. 

PNG: The JPEG and WebP limits do not apply, but Android Bitmap dimensions and available device memory still limit the result. 

Changing the output format does not reduce the memory required by decoded source images or the stitching canvas. If processing fails, first reduce the number or dimensions of the input images. Bitmap processing generally requires about 2-4 bytes per pixel, in addition to source, intermediate, preview, and output bitmaps.

Output Format: Select JPEG, PNG, or WebP according to compatibility, transparency, and encoder dimension requirements. \n\nOutput Quality: Applies only to JPEG and WebP. Higher values generally retain more detail and produce larger files. PNG output is not affected by this setting.

Multithreading: Allows independent image decoding and scaling work to run concurrently with a bounded number of Kotlin coroutine workers. The benefit depends on image size, CPU cores, and available memory; small workloads may not improve. \n\nIncrease Memory Threshold: Raises Chimera\'s preflight limit for the output canvas from 50% to 80% of reported device memory. It does not add RAM and can increase the risk of process termination or an out-of-memory failure.

Auto Clear Selected Images: Removes completed items from the selection list after stitching. It does not delete the source files. 

Storage Access Framework (SAF): Selects files through the system document provider without broad media-library access. 

Embedded Picker: Provides album browsing and search through components derived from ImageToolbox. It requires the applicable media permission, and the initial media index may take time to load. The system Photo Picker can still be used when broad media access is not desired.
